expectations.text: Recognizes auditEvent as a transactional / continuously-growing table
expectations.evidence: 'auditEvent is the textbook transactional table: append-only, timestamped, written by every service, unbounded growth'

expectations.text: Flags the missing time/date window (no bound on createdAt) and recommends one on an indexed column
expectations.evidence: Finding 2: no time window; add from/to default 7 days + index (type, createdAt DESC)

expectations.text: Flags the absence of a take/limit (unbounded result set) and recommends pagination (cursor/keyset or take)
expectations.evidence: Finding 1: no take returns every row; keyset cursor + capped take

expectations.text: Flags over-fetching via include (user + metadata) and recommends selecting only needed fields
expectations.evidence: Finding 4: include pulls full user+metadata; use select, move metadata to detail endpoint

expectations.text: States a tradeoff for at least one recommendation (not just the fix)
expectations.evidence: Tradeoffs on keyset (no page jumps), default window (behavior change), composite index (write cost)

expectations.text: Written in English
expectations.evidence: Entire review in English

expectations.text: Identifies func.lower(User.email) as a function on the column that prevents using the existing email index (forces a sequential scan)
expectations.evidence: Finding 1: lower() on column disables unique index -> seq scan of 12M rows (smell #2)

expectations.text: Explains the existing B-tree index is on the raw email value, so it cannot serve lower(email)
expectations.evidence: 'unique index is built over the stored value of email; comparing lower(users.email) can't probe by value'

expectations.text: Recommends a concrete fix: a functional/expression index on lower(email), normalizing email on write, or citext
expectations.evidence: Option to drop lower() if normalized; CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... (lower(email)); or citext

expectations.text: States the tradeoff of the recommended fix (e.g. functional index taxes writes, or normalization needs a migration)
expectations.evidence: Tradeoff: raw-compare only correct if write path guarantees lowercase; functional index = extra write/disk; citext = column migration; use CONCURRENTLY

expectations.text: Written in English
expectations.evidence: Entire review in English

expectations.text: Identifies that joins(:line_items) is 1:N and row-explodes the listing, so limit(20) returns 20 joined rows rather than 20 distinct orders
expectations.evidence: Finding 1: INNER JOIN one row per line item; @orders shows duplicates, AR won't dedup without .distinct

expectations.text: Flags that scope.count reuses the joined/ordered listing scope, making the count both wrong (counts join rows) and expensive
expectations.evidence: Finding 1+2: count counts line items not orders -> inflated on big accounts; reuses join+order

expectations.text: Recommends counting distinct orders on a lean scope without the 1:N join (and without the ORDER BY)
expectations.evidence: Lean base scope: @total = scope.count = COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE account_id; EXISTS if filter needed

expectations.text: Flags offset-based pagination degrading on deep pages and/or recommends keyset pagination
expectations.evidence: Finding 3: OFFSET reads+discards; keyset cursor on created_at

expectations.text: States a tradeoff for at least one recommendation
expectations.evidence: Tradeoffs: keyset can't jump pages/needs tiebreaker; exact COUNT still scans -> approximate option

expectations.text: Flags the absence of a take/limit (unbounded result set) and recommends pagination (cursor/keyset or take)

expectations.text: Flags over-fetching via include (user + metadata) and recommends selecting only needed fields

expectations.text: Identifies func.lower(User.email) as a function on the column that prevents using the existing email index (forces a sequential scan)
expectations.evidence: 'non-sargable predicate'; wrapped column -> sequential scan evaluating lower() on 12M rows

expectations.text: Explains the existing B-tree index is on the raw email value, so it cannot serve lower(email)
expectations.evidence: 'expression in the index (email) and in WHERE (lower(email)) do not match'

expectations.text: Recommends a concrete fix: a functional/expression index on lower(email), normalizing email on write, or citext
expectations.evidence: Option A functional index / citext; Option B normalize on write and drop lower()

expectations.text: States the tradeoff of the recommended fix (e.g. functional index taxes writes, or normalization needs a migration)
expectations.evidence: CONCURRENTLY to avoid lock; confirm no case-collisions; Option B requires normalize-on-write

expectations.text: Identifies that joins(:line_items) is 1:N and row-explodes the listing, so limit(20) returns 20 joined rows rather than 20 distinct orders
expectations.evidence: 'the list itself returns duplicates'; limit(20) selects from joined result, fewer than 20 distinct orders

expectations.text: Flags that scope.count reuses the joined/ordered listing scope, making the count both wrong (counts join rows) and expensive
expectations.evidence: Issue 1: COUNT(*) over join counts line items not orders; Issue 2 reuse + ORDER BY

expectations.text: Recommends counting distinct orders on a lean scope without the 1:N join (and without the ORDER BY)
expectations.evidence: base = Order.where(account_id); @total = base.count; or .distinct.count(:id) / EXISTS

expectations.text: Flags offset-based pagination degrading on deep pages and/or recommends keyset pagination
expectations.evidence: 'Pagination at scale': keyset/cursor instead of OFFSET which scans and discards skipped rows

expectations.text: States a tradeoff for at least one recommendation
expectations.evidence: 'If an exact total isn't essential, cache it / approximate / reltuples' — acknowledges cost of exact count
